title: Abstain when retrieval finds nothing relevant
description: When retrieval returns no sufficiently relevant passages, the system says it does not know rather than answering from parametric memory.
body: Use when the retriever may return weak or empty results. Do not fall back to the model's own knowledge silently, and avoid answering when the top passages are below a relevance threshold. Actions: set a relevance/score threshold, detect empty or low-confidence retrieval, and return an explicit 'no grounded answer' instead of guessing. Validation signals include a test that a query with no relevant corpus content yields abstention, not a fabricated answer. Risk boundary: answering from parametric memory while implying it came from the corpus is a trust violation; abstain and surface the gap.
